title: Dolphin Watching Boat Hire in Marbella: 2026 Costs & Tips
html:
For dolphin watching boat hire in Marbella, the best departures leave from Puerto Banús, with shared trips from EUR 45 per person and private yacht charters from EUR 400 for a half-day. Use a verified platform like BoatHire24 to compare trusted operators.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto Banús is the prime marina for dolphin watching charters, with many operators moored right on the Levante Quay. Marbella’s main port, Club del Mar, also offers easy access to the Costa del Sol waters.

Further east, Cabopino Marina provides a quieter launch point. The nearby Bay of Gibraltar, about 25 nautical miles south, is a known dolphin hotspot. Towns such as Estepona and Fuengirola also have marinas with boat hire options.

To secure the best options, consider a yacht charter Marbella via yacht charter Marbella for last-minute availability.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella for dolphin watching?

Shared dolphin watching tours start at EUR 45 per person, while private motorboat hire costs from EUR 400 for a half-day.

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours): EUR 45, 70 per person, includes guide and sometimes drinks.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 8 guests): EUR 400, 600, self-drive with licence.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): EUR 800, 1,500, with skipper and crew.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (up to 10 guests): EUR 2,000, 4,000, including amenities.

Which boat to rent for dolphin watching?

Choose a boat based on group size, budget, and whether you want a skipper or to drive yourself.

  1. Rib or rigid inflatable: best for speed and agility, up to 8 guests, from EUR 350 per half-day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess or Azimut): best for comfort and amenities, up to 12 guests, from EUR 800 per half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon): best for stability and space, up to 14 guests, from EUR 900 per half-day.
  4. Sailboat (e.g. Sun Odyssey): best for a quiet, eco-friendly experience, up to 6 guests, from EUR 400 per half-day.

For a top-end option, see the Princess F70 listing on BoatHire24 for full details and availability.

Season and licence requirements

The best months for dolphin watching are May through October, with calm seas and frequent sightings. The Poniente wind (west wind) often brings smooth conditions in summer. No licence is needed if you hire a boat with a skipper; for self-drive, a Spanish recreational boating licence is required, but the operator will confirm exact requirements.

In high season, book a morning departure for the calmest seas and best chance of seeing pods of bottlenose dolphins.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 45, 4,000 per person or boat
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 6, 14 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I see dolphins in Marbella all year round?

Dolphins are present all year, but sightings are most reliable from May to October when the sea is calmer.

Do I need to bring my own food on a dolphin watching charter?

Most private charters include drinks and snacks, and full-day trips often include a meal; check with the operator.

How far offshore do the boats go for dolphin watching?

Typical trips cover 5 to 15 nautical miles from the coast, often towards the Bay of Gibraltar or Cabopino.

What boat models are available for rent in Marbella?

You can rent Azimut, Princess, Fairline, Lagoon, and Sunseeker yachts, among others, through BoatHire24.
